name: Saddle Stitching
description: Hand-stitching method using two needles and waxed thread worked through pre-punched holes. Each stitch is an independent lock — stronger and more repairable than machine stitching.
steps: Mark stitch line with a wing divider set to 4mm from the edge.
Punch holes along the stitch line with a 4-prong stitching chisel.
Cut thread at 2.5× the stitch length and thread a needle on each end.
Push the first needle through hole 1 and pull to the midpoint.
Push the second needle through the same hole in the opposite direction.
Pull both ends tight and evenly; repeat for each hole.
Backstitch through the last 2 holes to lock, trim, and melt ends.
